issues.message: Focused control has no accessible name

evidence.reason: A keyboard user reached this control, but it has no label, text, aria-label, or other usable name.
evidence.name: preferred_course

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible label, text content, or aria-label so students know what the control does.

rule.title: Focused control has no accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A keyboard user can reach the control, but without a name a screen reader announces nothing useful about what it does.
rule.manual_review_notes: The accessible name is estimated; confirm with browser dev tools or a screen reader what is actually announced.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Accessible names are estimated from labels, text, and common attributes, not from a full accessibility tree computation.

issues.message: Focused control has no accessible name

evidence.reason: A keyboard user reached this control, but it has no label, text, aria-label, or other usable name.

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible label, text content, or aria-label so students know what the control does.

rule.title: Focused control has no accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A keyboard user can reach the control, but without a name a screen reader announces nothing useful about what it does.
rule.manual_review_notes: The accessible name is estimated; confirm with browser dev tools or a screen reader what is actually announced.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Accessible names are estimated from labels, text, and common attributes, not from a full accessibility tree computation.

issues.message: Form control is missing an accessible label

evidence.reason: Form control has no accessible label.
evidence.name: preferred_course

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible <label> connected with for/id. Use aria-label only when a visible label is not possible.

rule.title: Form control missing accessible label
rule.why_it_matters: Students using keyboard navigation, assistive technology, or simplified layouts may not know what information the field expects.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the visual design already implies the label but fails programmatically.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot always confirm labels created dynamically by JavaScript.

issues.message: Button is missing an accessible name

evidence.reason: Button has no visible text, aria-label, title, or image alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add clear button text or an aria-label that describes the button action.

rule.title: Button missing accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A button without a name is announced as just 'button' by screen readers, so students cannot tell what pressing it will do.
rule.manual_review_notes: Icon-only buttons may look meaningful visually but still need a programmatic name.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot see names added later by JavaScript or icon fonts.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.
Browser mode approximates keyboard interaction but does not simulate a full screen reader.
Accessible names are estimated and require manual review.
